factors.name: Tech Sector Weakness & AI Spending Concerns
factors.summary: Nasdaq fell 0.24% Friday and down 4.60% for the week; tech rout exposed $270B speculation machine as investors question ROI on AI capex. Oracle worst week since 2001 dot-com with negative FCF and $130B debt pile weighing heavily.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US Escalation)
factors.summary: US conducted retaliatory strikes on Iran after drone attack on cargo ship Ever Lovely in Strait of Hormuz; oil rebounded intraday but broader supply-chain disruption risk present. Uncertainty caps upside into event resolution.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse & Energy Input Costs
factors.summary: Crude settled at $69.23 (-3.74% day, -8.5% week, -42.6% from $119.48 peak); deflationary for Dow cyclicals and consumer-facing names but headwind for energy sector exposure. Longer-term easing inflation narrative is equity-supportive.

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: Fed Chair Warsh talking tough on inflation, helping coax Treasury yields lower; Kashkari projects one rate hike this year (not hawkish surprise). Lower nominal yields support equities and dividend-paying industrials in Dow.

factors.name: Treasury Yields Declining
factors.summary: Bond yields falling as inflation pressures ease post-oil collapse and Fed messaging turns less hawkish. Lower long-end rates support dividend payers (utilities, REITs) and reduce discount rate drag on Dow valuations.

factors.name: Dow Daily Performance & Momentum
factors.summary: Dow down 0.09% Friday; 4h change -0.19%, 24h change -0.20% — grinding lower into week-end with no clear bullish setup. Broader tech/AI unwind pulling down Nasdaq harder but Dow lagging, not leading.

factors.name: Cyclical & Industrials Sentiment
factors.summary: No earnings surprises or forward guidance from Dow heavyweights (CAT, BA, HON, DE, MMM) in this news window. On Semi down 6% worst day since 2020; mixed signals on physical AI pivot.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: Greenback ends mixed for week; no major DXY move noted in data. Multinational revenue translation for Dow names stable in near term; no currency crisis signaling.

factors.name: China Economic Data & Trade Policy
factors.summary: No fresh China PMI or growth data in window; Trump threatens tariffs on digital tax countries but USMCA renegotiations ongoing. Trade friction remains structural headwind, not acute catalyst this session.

factors.name: Consumer & Healthcare Rotation
factors.summary: Healthcare stocks (AbbVie, Eli Lilly, J&J) tracking to all-time highs as investors rotate from tech; UNH, JNJ in Dow provide defensive anchor as growth names stumble.

factors.name: Upcoming US Jobs Report (Next Week)
factors.summary: NFP coming next week (end of June week); markets likely to chop and remain defensive into labor data. No consensus shift yet on employment strength vs. recession concerns.

key_levels.resistance: 52100–52200 (prior week's close area, needs clear bullish catalyst to break)

screening.narrative: The Dow finished Friday and the week in a modest drawdown as a broader tech/AI rout (Nasdaq -4.60% weekly) dragged sentiment despite some defensive rotation into healthcare and lower Treasury yields. Geopolitical escalation with Iran-US strikes added near-term uncertainty, and oil's 42% plunge from peak is deflationary but also signals demand concerns. The Fed's softer stance from Warsh helped yields retreat, supporting dividend yields and lowering equity discount rates — a positive for the Dow's industrial and defensive composition — but this has not yet translated into fresh upside conviction. The index is in Phase 2: a minor move down is underway with no strong retest of resistance yet.
catalysts_ahead: US Jobs Report (NFP) — end of next week; labor market test for Fed taper/hike path
ISM Manufacturing PMI (next week) — key read for Dow's industrial-heavy constituents (CAT, BA, MMM, HON, GE)
ISM Services PMI (next week) — broader economic health for consumer-facing Dow names
Dow Component Earnings Releases (rolling through July) — UNH, MSFT, GS, V, JPM guidance will reset price targets
FOMC Meeting (mid-July) — Fed policy signaling; Warsh's inflation messaging will be tested
China economic data (July) — June PMI and growth updates; impact on Caterpillar, Boeing, materials demand
screening.risk_warning: If Iran-US tensions escalate further (broader shipping disruption, energy spike) or if Dow heavyweights report earnings misses tied to demand weakness (esp. industrials, financials), downside accelerates. Conversely, a stronger-than-expected NFP, Fed signals of pause/eventual cuts, or tech sector stabilization would quickly flip bias BULLISH and invalidate the neutral stance.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is extended down into week-end; wait for Monday retest of 51600–51700 support zone before considering re-entry on long side. Alternatively, watch for clearance above 52100 resistance on fresh bullish catalyst (e.g., dovish Fed commentary, Chinese stimulus) — do not chase down here.
